definition: A level in a design system that separates raw tokens from component-level decisions.
exampleSentence: The abstraction layer separates raw token values from component-level decisions.

definition: A semantic token that references a primitive token and communicates its intended use.
exampleSentence: An alias token like color-primary just points to blue-600 under the hood.

definition: A methodology by Brad Frost organizing UI into atoms, molecules, organisms, templates, and pages.
exampleSentence: We follow atomic design principles, starting with atoms and building up to pages.

definition: An unstyled or minimally styled component that provides behavior without visual opinions.
exampleSentence: The base component provides the right keyboard behavior without any visual styling.

definition: A horizontal rhythm system aligning text and elements to a consistent vertical spacing unit.
exampleSentence: All text in the interface snaps to the baseline grid for consistent vertical rhythm.

definition: A design token defining the corner rounding values available across a system.
exampleSentence: Use the border radius token so corners stay consistent across every card.

definition: A defined viewport width at which the layout adapts to suit a different screen size.
exampleSentence: The sidebar collapses at the mobile breakpoint to save screen space.

definition: A CSS @layer rule that explicitly controls the order of specificity between style groups.
exampleSentence: We use a cascade layer to ensure utility classes always win over component defaults.

definition: The complete set of raw color values available in a system before semantic assignment.
exampleSentence: The full color palette has twelve hues with ten shades each.

definition: A graduated scale of tints and shades for a single hue, typically numbered 50 through 900.
exampleSentence: The blue color ramp goes from blue-50 for backgrounds to blue-900 for text.

definition: A semantic label describing a color's function — such as surface, on-surface, or error.
exampleSentence: The error color role is used wherever the system needs to signal a problem.

definition: The set of props a component exposes to control its appearance and behavior from outside.
exampleSentence: Keep the component API small so consuming teams have less to learn.

definition: A versioned, documented collection of reusable UI components shared across a product or org.
exampleSentence: Every product team pulls from the shared component library instead of building their own.

definition: Building complex UI by combining smaller, focused components rather than creating monolithic ones.
exampleSentence: We build complex layouts through composition of smaller focused components.

definition: A style rule applied only when two or more variant conditions are true simultaneously.
exampleSentence: A compound variant applies a special style only when size is small and variant is ghost.

definition: A deliberate system rule that limits choices to maintain consistency across the product.
exampleSentence: A spacing constraint means teams can only pick from eight approved values.

definition: A product team that uses a design system's components without contributing to its core.
exampleSentence: The consuming team opened an issue because the button padding changed unexpectedly.

definition: A variable declared with -- prefix in CSS, used to propagate design token values at runtime.
exampleSentence: The theme color is set via a CSS custom property on the root element.

definition: An approach colocating component styles written in JavaScript alongside component logic.
exampleSentence: We moved away from CSS-in-JS to reduce runtime overhead in the bundle.

definition: A color scheme variant using dark backgrounds, requiring semantic tokens to switch correctly.
exampleSentence: Semantic tokens make dark mode work without touching component code.

definition: The amount of content and spacing in a UI — compact vs comfortable vs spacious layouts.
exampleSentence: The compact density option reduces padding for data-heavy admin screens.

definition: The shared visual and interactive vocabulary — principles, patterns, and aesthetics — of a brand.
exampleSentence: The new design language is warmer and rounder than the previous version.

definition: A named variable storing a single design decision — color, spacing, radius — usable across platforms.
exampleSentence: Every color, size, and radius in the UI is stored as a design token.

definition: A dedicated website publishing component usage guidelines, props, and live code examples.
exampleSentence: The documentation site shows live examples of every component variant.

definition: A system representing depth through shadow values, communicating component stacking order.
exampleSentence: Modals sit at the highest elevation level in the shadow token scale.

definition: A secondary typeface rendered while a web font loads to prevent invisible or shifted text.
exampleSentence: A well-matched fallback font prevents layout shift while the brand font loads.

definition: Font sizes that scale smoothly between breakpoints using CSS clamp() instead of fixed steps.
exampleSentence: Fluid typography scales headings smoothly between mobile and desktop without steps.

definition: A visible outline appearing on focused interactive elements to aid keyboard navigation.
exampleSentence: The focus ring must be visible enough to meet WCAG contrast requirements.

definition: A set of predefined font size steps — xs through 4xl — forming the typographic range of a system.
exampleSentence: The type scale defines seven sizes from xs through 4xl for consistent hierarchy.

definition: An ordered list of typefaces in CSS, falling back to the next if the preferred one is unavailable.
exampleSentence: The font stack falls back to system-ui when the brand font isn't available.

definition: CSS rules applied to the entire document — resets, root variables, and base element defaults.
exampleSentence: Global styles set the root font size and CSS custom properties for the theme.

definition: Fixed spacing between columns in a grid layout that maintains visual separation.
exampleSentence: Consistent gutters between columns keep the layout breathable at every breakpoint.

definition: A component providing only logic and accessibility with no default styles applied.
exampleSentence: A headless component gives you accessible dialog behavior without any default styles.

definition: A CSS custom property storing a hue value to drive a dynamic accent color system.
exampleSentence: Changing one hue token updates the entire accent color across the product.

definition: A cohesive set of SVG icons sharing consistent sizing, stroke weight, and visual style.
exampleSentence: Every icon in the icon system uses the same 24px grid and 1.5px stroke.

definition: The ability of a child component to receive token values cascaded from a parent theme.
exampleSentence: Token inheritance lets a child theme override just a few values from the parent.

definition: A component's visual condition in response to user action: hover, focus, active, or disabled.
exampleSentence: Design the interactive state for hover, focus, and disabled before handoff.

definition: A semantic surface token — background, surface, overlay — defining stacking context colors.
exampleSentence: The surface layer token switches from white to dark gray in dark mode.

definition: The vertical space allocated for each line of text, affecting readability and vertical rhythm.
exampleSentence: A line height of 1.5 is comfortable for body text at most font sizes.

definition: Automated checks enforcing that only approved design tokens are used in component code.
exampleSentence: Token linting fails the build if a hardcoded color sneaks into a component.

definition: An ARIA attribute that announces dynamic content changes to screen readers automatically.
exampleSentence: The toast message uses a live region so screen readers announce it automatically.

definition: A design token storing duration and easing values for consistent animation across a system.
exampleSentence: All transitions use a motion token so the whole UI feels consistently paced.

definition: Adjusting letter-spacing or weight at different sizes to maintain visual balance of text.
exampleSentence: Optical sizing tightens letter spacing at large display sizes to look balanced.

definition: A documented, reusable solution to a recurring design problem within a specific context.
exampleSentence: The confirmation dialog is a documented pattern teams should reuse everywhere.

definition: A component that can render as different HTML elements via an 'as' prop without losing its styles.
exampleSentence: A polymorphic component can render as a button or an anchor depending on the as prop.

definition: A raw, context-free token storing a single value — blue-500, space-4 — without semantic meaning.
exampleSentence: Primitive tokens like blue-500 have no opinion about where they should be used.

definition: Applying different styles to a component based on the values of its received props.
exampleSentence: Prop-driven styling keeps variants in one place instead of scattered across CSS files.

definition: A CSS media query respecting the user's OS preference to minimize animation and transitions.
exampleSentence: Respect the reduced motion preference by disabling decorative animations.

definition: A stylesheet neutralizing browser default styles to create a consistent cross-browser baseline.
exampleSentence: The CSS reset removes browser defaults so every element starts from the same baseline.

definition: A token whose value changes at defined breakpoints to adapt spacing or type for screen size.
exampleSentence: A responsive token increases the spacing value from four to eight on wider screens.

definition: A set of predefined spacing values — 4, 8, 12, 16… — used consistently across layout and components.
exampleSentence: The spacing scale uses multiples of four so layouts always align to a grid.

definition: A color token named by its purpose — danger, success, muted — rather than its raw value.
exampleSentence: Use the semantic color token for danger, not the raw red hex value.

definition: A design token encoding box-shadow values to represent consistent elevation levels.
exampleSentence: The card uses the low shadow token and the modal uses the high shadow token.

definition: A named placeholder inside a component where consumers inject custom content or child elements.
exampleSentence: The card component has a slot for custom actions in the footer area.

definition: A numeric weight CSS assigns to selectors to decide which rule wins when rules conflict.
exampleSentence: A high specificity score on a utility class can be hard to override later.

definition: An open-source tool that transforms design tokens into platform-specific output files.
exampleSentence: Style Dictionary transforms the token JSON into CSS variables and iOS constants.

definition: Swapping a set of token values to change the visual appearance of a system without touching components.
exampleSentence: Theming lets each brand swap colors and fonts without touching any component.

definition: The automated workflow transforming raw token definitions into CSS variables or JS exports.
exampleSentence: The token pipeline runs on every merge and publishes a new CSS variables file.

definition: A single-purpose CSS class applying one specific style property, as used in Tailwind CSS.
exampleSentence: A utility class like flex applies a single CSS property in one place.

definition: A named style configuration of a component — primary, ghost, destructive — controlled via props.
exampleSentence: The destructive variant of the button is red and reserved for dangerous actions.

definition: Tagging system releases with semantic version numbers to manage breaking and non-breaking changes.
exampleSentence: Breaking changes in the component library always bump the major version number.

definition: An automated test comparing component screenshots to detect unintended visual changes.
exampleSentence: A visual regression test caught a shadow change that reviewers missed.

definition: A set of named z-index token values — dropdown, modal, toast — to manage stacking order predictably.
exampleSentence: The z-index scale has named levels like dropdown and modal to avoid magic numbers.

definition: Class Variance Authority — a library for defining typed component variants with Tailwind CSS.
exampleSentence: CVA lets you define all button variants in one typed object instead of scattered classes.

definition: Resolving Tailwind conflicts when multiple classes target the same CSS property, using tools like cn().
exampleSentence: Use class merging with cn() so consumer classes override the component defaults.

definition: The rate at which product teams migrate to and consistently use a shared design system.
exampleSentence: Adoption of the new system reached seventy percent of teams within six months.

definition: The defined process for how teams outside the core system team can propose or add components.
exampleSentence: The contribution model lets product teams propose new components through a RFC.

definition: An attribute communicating an element's purpose to assistive technologies beyond its HTML tag.
exampleSentence: Add the correct ARIA role so screen readers announce the element properly.

definition: A React API that passes a ref through a component to a DOM element inside it.
exampleSentence: Wrap the input with forwardRef so parent components can access the DOM node.

definition: A component is open for extension via props but closed for modification of its internal logic.
exampleSentence: The open/closed principle means you extend a component with props, not internal edits.

definition: Making minor visual adjustments — often misalignments of 1–2px — to achieve precise rendering.
exampleSentence: After all the logic was done, we spent an afternoon pixel pushing the spacing.

definition: A semantic color role representing the background layer on which content and components sit.
exampleSentence: Place content on the surface token color so it adapts correctly to dark mode.
